commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7fd62deaa2f02f4620dae7c6b60ef0fc858bfa3f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Matrix encrypted-event retry handling so exhausted missing-key decryptions can be requeued on later Matrix crypto key signals, with Matrix SDK and monitor regression tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +95. Total +117 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. not as a high-confidence live reproduction. Source inspection and the PR's RED/GREEN test support the current-main failure path, but live Docker or Matrix homeserver recovery has not been reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because live Matrix proof remains a merge gate, and the branch also has a narrow retained-state cleanup defect.

target.title: fix #93501: [Bug]: Matrix error decrypting event
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: issue #93501 reports Matrix E2EE missing-key decrypt failures that can stop practical message delivery until the bot account is opened in Element and the gateway is restarted.
- Solution: repair the Matrix failed-decryption retry bridge so exhausted missing-key events can be retried when later Matrix crypto key signals arrive.
- What changed: `decrypt-bridge.ts` stores exhausted retry state and Matrix crypto key signals requeue that state with attempts reset; the earlier missing-key string special case in sync lifecycle is removed.
- What did NOT change: Matrix login, auth, provider routing, session storage, Matrix wire format, and ordinary `sync.unexpected_error` fatal-stop behavior.
- Why it matters / User impact: a Matrix room key can arrive after OpenClaw has already exhausted per-event decrypt retries; without requeueing that event, the channel can remain unable to deliver the message until manual Element login plus gateway restart.
- Fixes #93501

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — this updates the existing PR after ClawSweeper identified that the first lifecycle-level fix targeted the wrong layer.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** #94044 is referenced by daily-fix as a skipped pull-request ref for the same linked issue context.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** This update is for the current open contributor PR #94416 and specifically addresses ClawSweeper's author blocker on this PR: prove the SDK path or move the fix to failed-decryption handling.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** the patch no longer depends on a downstream log-shaped string check in the monitor lifecycle. It changes the Matrix failed-decryption retry owner, where exhausted event retry state is created and where crypto key update signals are already handled.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** the regression exercises OpenClaw's MatrixClient event bridge and retry scheduler instead of manually emitting a synthetic lifecycle `sync.unexpected_error`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Matrix encrypted events that previously exhausted the failed-decryption retry cap can be retried when a later Matrix crypto key signal indicates keys may now be available. Ordinary Matrix sync lifecycle failures remain fatal and visible.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw Matrix extension test environment in `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-94416`, using the repository MatrixClient event bridge and dependency source evidence from `matrix-js-sdk@41.6.0` unpacked under `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94416-evidence/matrix-js-sdk-41.6.0`. No live Matrix homeserver credentials are available in this environment.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-94416 && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/sync-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/index.test.ts --testTimeout=300000
git -C /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-94416 diff --check
DAILY_FIX=/home/0668001029/.claude/skills/daily-fix/scripts/daily_fix.sh DAILY_FIX_CONTEXT=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94416-evidence/context.env "$DAILY_FIX" record-verification /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94416-evidence/matrix-decrypt-retry-local-verification.txt /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94416-evidence/matrix-js-sdk-41.6.0/lib/models/event.js /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94416-evidence/matrix-js-sdk-41.6.0/lib/sync.js
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Matrix SDK + monitor suites:
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-matrix.config.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       136 passed (136)
Duration    52.09s
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 69.69s

git diff --check:
PASS with no output

record-verification:
ok: true
verified_base_head: abd8ca899b239874dfff563879773ea30ac5c6b7
pending_diff_sha256: b44828979470bc4245ff19e7354b88e890ca13e66d12f869d00f4f9a6f95869d
committed_head: 7fd62deaa2f02f4620dae7c6b60ef0fc858bfa3f
verification marker: /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94416-evidence/verification-marker.json
```

Dependency-backed Matrix SDK path check:

```text
matrix-js-sdk@41.6.0 lib/models/event.js:720 defines MatrixEvent.decryptionLoop.
matrix-js-sdk@41.6.0 lib/models/event.js:771 logs "Error decrypting event (...): ..." on decrypt failure.
matrix-js-sdk@41.6.0 lib/models/event.js:793 emits MatrixEventEvent.Decrypted with the decrypt error.
matrix-js-sdk@41.6.0 lib/sync.js:770 logs "Caught /sync error" only around processSyncResponse exceptions.
matrix-js-sdk@41.6.0 lib/sync.js:773 emits ClientEvent.SyncUnexpectedError from that /sync exception path.
```

RED check before the bridge fix, in a temporary test-only scratch worktree:

```text
Command: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.test.ts --testNamePattern "retries an exhausted missing-key decryption when a crypto key signal arrives"
Result before fix: failed as expected
Failure: AssertionError: expected "vi.fn()" to be called 1 times, but got 0 times
Meaning: after retry exhaustion, crypto.keyBackupDecryptionKeyCached did not requeue the missing-key event.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** after the retry cap is exhausted, firing `crypto.keyBackupDecryptionKeyCached` requeues the encrypted event, calls `decryptEventIfNeeded` again, and delivers the decrypted `m.room.message`. A missing-key-shaped error emitted through `sync.unexpected_error` is still recorded as fatal, so fatal sync failures are not hidden.
- **What was not tested:** I could not run a live Matrix homeserver / Element-account proof because this environment has no Matrix account credentials or live homeserver access. This proof is dependency-backed source analysis plus local Matrix SDK bridge behavior; live Matrix key-backup behavior still needs maintainer or ClawSweeper environment validation.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Live homeserver proof remains a disclosed maintainer/ClawSweeper validation gap; the code direction is now backed by matrix-js-sdk source evidence showing the logged missing-key path is event decryption, not a proven lifecycle `sync.unexpected_error` path.
- **RF-002:** Fixed by moving the recovery behavior to `decrypt-bridge.ts` and preserving lifecycle fatal behavior for ordinary sync errors.
- **RF-003:** Fixed locally with a MatrixClient event-bridge regression that starts the client, emits an encrypted Matrix event, exhausts decrypt retries, fires a Matrix crypto key signal, and observes delivery after re-decryption.
- **RF-004:** Fixed by using matrix-js-sdk v41.6.0 source evidence to reject the manual log-shaped lifecycle event assumption.
- **RF-005:** Fixed by removing the lifecycle exception that could have hidden future fatal sync diagnostics.
- **RF-006:** Fixed by keeping `sync.unexpected_error` fatal and adding a negative-control lifecycle test.
- **RF-007:** Maintainer decision still needed for live Matrix proof; the patch now gives source-level path evidence, local RED/GREEN proof, and a narrowed risk boundary.
- **RF-008:** Stale for the new patch direction: the logged decrypt error no longer needs to reach the lifecycle because the lifecycle special case was removed.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test files:** `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.test.ts`, `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/sync-lifecycle.test.ts`, and `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/index.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** an encrypted Matrix event exhausts the failed-decryption retry cap, then `crypto.keyBackupDecryptionKeyCached` fires after keys may be available; the bridge retries the event and delivers the decrypted `m.room.message`.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** it exercises OpenClaw's MatrixClient event bridge, decrypt retry state, and crypto-signal listener without requiring private Matrix credentials. The lifecycle test is a separate negative control that prevents reintroducing the downstream string-based nonfatal exception.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** message-delivery / session-state / security-boundary.
- **Risk explanation:** message delivery and retry state are touched because exhausted failed-decryption entries are now retained with their retry state for later crypto-key requeue. The security-boundary signal is from retaining encrypted-event retry state inside the Matrix decrypt bridge, not from changing keys, credentials, auth, encryption algorithms, or Matrix wire data. The `waitPromise.catch(() => undefined)` line is test cleanup after intentionally rejecting the lifecycle fatal wait, not production broad error handling.
- **Why acceptable:** the change is limited to the Matrix failed-decryption retry owner, resets attempts only when Matrix crypto key signals fire, preserves duplicate in-flight retry protection, and keeps fatal sync errors visible. Adjacent retry-cap, duplicate-signal, immediate-signal, exhausted-requeue, and lifecycle tests passed together in the Matrix shard.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** OpenClaw removed exhausted failed-decryption retries from `decryptRetries` and kept only a retry key in `exhaustedDecryptRetries`. When Matrix later emitted a crypto key update signal, `retryPendingNow()` could only retry entries still in `decryptRetries`, so the event that needed the newly available key stayed abandoned until an external restart or fresh event flow rebuilt state.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** the fix preserves the source retry state at the point where retries are exhausted and requeues that state when Matrix reports key material may have changed. It repairs the owner of the failed-decryption retry invariant instead of masking the symptom in monitor lifecycle error handling.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high: the local source-level path and MatrixClient event-bridge proof are strong, but live Matrix homeserver proof is unavailable here and remains disclosed above.
- **Patch quality notes:** The retry behavior change is covered by focused RED/GREEN proof and adjacent retry guardrails. No fallback, silent swallow, `ts-ignore`, eslint-disable, or lifecycle string special case remains.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth owner is `MatrixDecryptBridge`, which owns failed-decryption retry state and crypto key retry signals. This patch changes that owner before downstream monitor lifecycle projection. Compatibility is preserved for public Matrix SDK event behavior: `sync.unexpected_error` remains fatal, no Matrix SDK API surface is changed, and no config/schema/protocol defaults are changed.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
